lecture_guidance: Count missing values by column before deciding what to do.
Deletes rows with any NaN and returns a copy unless assigned/inplace.
when_it_fits: Small number of non-essential missing rows.

lecture_guidance: Deletes columns with any NaN and returns a copy unless assigned/inplace.
when_it_fits: Columns are optional and mostly incomplete.
risk: Can remove important features such as rolling averages.

lecture_guidance: Possible but dangerous for price data.
when_it_fits: Only when zero is economically meaningful.

lecture_guidance: Preferred lecture default for measures when no new information is available.
when_it_fits: Time series prices or state variables.
risk: First value still remains NaN; stale values can persist through long gaps.

lecture_guidance: Discussed as a better alternative in some cases, using only past data.
when_it_fits: Noisy metrics where recent history is a reasonable estimate.
risk: Window choice matters; do not use future data.
